children.name: Orthonyx
children.description: A genus of unusual, ground-dwelling passerines endemic to eastern Australia and New Guinea, with a peculiar rigid tail structure adapted for bracing against the ground. Their legs are exceptionally powerful, used to sweep aside leaf litter in a distinctive sideways motion as they forage for invertebrates. They are among the most primitive living songbirds.

children.name: Orthonyx temminckii
children.description: A stocky, ground-dwelling bird with a short tail and powerful legs, found in the rainforests of eastern Australia. It uses a unique foraging technique, sweeping its head sideways to toss leaf litter aside and expose invertebrates. Its loud, explosive call consists of a series of harsh, metallic notes.

children.name: Orthonyx spaldingii
children.description: A large, dark logrunner with a chestnut belly and throat, restricted to the rainforests of northeastern Queensland. Like its relative the Australian Logrunner, it sweeps leaf litter with powerful leg kicks to find prey. It is named for its distinctive territorial call, 'chow-chow-chilla', which resonates through the mountain rainforests.
